在整個人類歷史上，強者，富人和具有狡猾特質的人捕食部落，氏族，城鎮，城市和鄉村中的弱者，無`'守和貧窮成員。然而，人類的
生存意願迫使那些被拒絕，被剝奪或摧毀的基本需求的人們找到了一種生活方式，並繼續將其A融入不斷發展的人類社會。
說到食物，不要以為那些被拒絕的人只吃垃圾。相反，他們學會了在被忽視的肉類和蔬菜中尋找營養。他們學會了清潔，切塊，調味和
慢燉慢燉的野菜和肉類，在食品市場上被忽略的部分家用蔬菜和肉類，並且學會了使用芳香的木煙（如山核桃，山核桃和豆科灌木）來調味食物煮的時候:"

Throughout the entire movie she's portrayed as a badass who doesn't trust anyone and the dragon is portrayed as naive and "everyone can be trusted if given the chance". They do meet a few other characters who tag along for the adventure. The setting is beautiful, the animation is top notch, the voice acting is excellent, but the story itself is the same re-hashed stuff we've seen from Disney over and over again, however that doesn't necessarily make it a bad movie because it works pretty well in this instance.

Kinda made me emotional. I came from a 12 hour shift and went to the cinemas just to see this. The young girl I was needed to see a disney princess who looked like me. And the fact that there are so many elements that are so reminiscent of my culture just warms my heart. 

Btw, it was a requirement in my highschool(in the Philippines) to study Raya's martial arts. It's called Arnis-cali-eskrima. I just want to give a shoutout to Jil, my arnis sparring partner. Im sorry for breaking your nose.

Raya and the Last Dragon is a synergy of Southeast Asian cultures with learning to trust as its key element. Bravo! I really love this movie.

PHILIPPINES KULTURE
RAYA IN MINDANAO REGION

It's a beautiful movie. Happy to see a warrior princess, not with a deep set of eyes or monolid eyes but with almond eyes. Princess with dark brown skin that loves to eat rice. Another empowered warrior princesses with no prince charming.  Has trust issues but chooses to have fate with friendship. Dragons that looks like unicorn and care bears at the same time. 

She fought for her father and their family to live. Used Arnis and different Southeast Asian fight styles and swords. The legendary dragon chooses to be "hospitable" by giving a gift to a stranger or clan leader instead of attacking the enemy like what usually dragons do in movies. A warrior princess wears "Salakot" (A Filipino traditional headgear) and has a pet named tuk-tuk, colorful ethnic clothes, stilt houses, friends bonded with spicy rice soup, very Southeast Asian. And some peeps said it's a combination of other Disney princesses movies, some people. So different from avatar and not even close to Moana or not even the Boss baby lol. #Disney, you nailed it.

One thing I was surprised by is how many times a character gets *totally screwed* by trusting a stranger. Even more surprising, in some of those cases, the film also gives a nod to the reasons for why a stranger might take those unkind actions. Poverty, wealth inequality, history, cultural differences, personal biases, and misunderstanding are all real-world reasons for why people are not always going to treat each other as brother and sister. Raya is just a Disney fantasy film aimed at kids, it's not aiming for the complexity or moral ambiguity of Game of Thrones or House of Cards or anything like that. But it does actually provide a somewhat believable setting where people refuse to trust each other and as a result must suffer the terrible fate of being in constant conflict.

Dragons are not really a SEA thing. SEA dragons are more like serpents. They're more prominent in indochina, but not much elsewhere. For example, in the Malay archipelago, the "main" mythical creature is the garuda (thunderbird).

The reason the producers chose dragon is because it fits better with Western audiences expectations of the Far East. Also the look and feel of the production skews more East Asian. It doesn't really look like SE Asia. 

Still, to have a major production set in the region is progress, I guess. SEA is grossly underrepresented in comparison to EA.

No, that's not correct. Frozen, Moana, and Raya are all produced by "Walt Disney Animation" (WD Animation), which was not involved in Mulan or any of the live action remakes.
The live action remakes are all produced by "Walt Disney Pictures" (WD Pictures), and unlike Pixar or WD Animation movies, the live action films are usually co-productions with other companies. In the case of Mulan, it was a co-production between WD Pictures, Jason T. Reed Productions, and Good Fear Productions. As a result, the live action remakes have a lot less consistency in production than anything coming out of WD Animation or Pixar.
That being said, all the films are distributed by Disney...But distribution (in theory) should have the least amount of artistic input on the film itself. In practice the amount of control each party has varies a lot from film to film.

The Asian symbolism is so appropriate considering dragons are so iconic in Eastern culture. Unlike the quadrupedal fire-breathers of Western folklore, Eastern dragons were benevolent, slender and serpentine with only two legs in front and occasionally having thin, spindly mustaches.  They would also deliver rain so as to fertilize crops and would only unleash their wrath if angered.

Music

James Newton Howard will compose the score for Raya and the Last Dragon.[1] The film marks the fourth time he has scored an animated film by Walt Disney Animation Studios, having previously composed for Dinosaur, Atlantis: The Lost Empire, and Treasure Planet. The score was released on February 26, 2021. Jhené Aiko wrote and performed a song for the end-credits, titled "Lead the Way".[26]

On March 2, 2021, Disney Studios Philippines announced that Filipina singer KZ Tandingan will be singing Disney’s first-ever Filipino-language song, titled "Gabay",[27] which means "guide" in English. The track will be part of the film's soundtrack. Allie Benedicto, studio marketing head of Disney Philippines said that "Gabay", the first-ever Disney track sung in Filipino, "demonstrates our commitment to work with local creative talents to tell our stories in a locally relevant manner."[28

I have a bad feeling about this movie, I can't pin down why. It's visually stunning, it's trying something new, it seems to be taking world building more seriously, it's made by the studio that made Moana, these are all good qualities. But the little voice in my head is screaming "This movie is gonna suck!" I hope I'm proven wrong, but these trailers feel like a hardcore pandering attempt to be "cool". I'm worried that all the jokes and story beats will not land and just feel stupid, but regardless of whether the movie sucks or not, if it has one good song in it the Disney fanbase will hold it on a pedastal for the rest of my life, so I will just have to suck it up and pretend like it's a good movie.

I know Jasmine is Arabian/Middle Easterners, but I think she is also partially influenced by Indian culture.
The clothing. The palace resembles Taj Mahal. Jasmine lives in Agrabah, which name similar to Agra. Her pet is Rajah, which is a Bengal tiger. Etc.

And also, Disney seems to like mingling many cultures to make a movie. In this case, Raya is inspired by many cultures of Southeast Asia. Similarly, Moana was a combination of all Polynesian country cultures. So, probably Aladdin (and Jasmine) also took many influences from Arab/Middle East and Indian Subcontinent. But yeah, it would be cool if Disney make a movie specifically based on India.

In the trailer, we see that people were turned into stone by the Druun, probably including Raya’s father and Namaari’s mother. What kind of monster the Druun is? We don’t see it yet in the trailer. I have a feeling that the Druun would be scary.
Only a creature I know of that has that power in Southeast Asia mythology is Sang Kelembai (its name in Malaysia).
@nat_lysa320  wow. So, the Druun is probably based on Sang Kelembai. Interesting!

@taufiqindrarukmana We have actually a lot more folktales about people turning to stone but many of these stories are to punish those with bad intentions.

Like stories of Si Tanggal and The Crying Stone (A West Kalimantan tale), the characters turned into stones because they disrespected their mothers.
In Si Pahit Lidah, the character was punished because of his jealousy.

I think the Druun has this intention to punish the bad and the people of Kumandra probably did something that damage the nature or the people are just bitter to each other.

@nat_lysa320  I see. I also remember a story of Malin Kundang from West Sumatra, who were cursed into stone because he defied her mother. There is also a tale from Central Java about Roro Jonggrang. She were turned into stone after deceiving Bandung Bondowoso, who wanted to marry her.
@taufiqindrarukmana Ah, thanks for reminding me the name Malin Kundang, Si Tanggal is the Malaysian name to the story.

The dragon’s name being Sisu is also strange as it’s a Finnish word (there is no direct translation but it basically means inner peace) and I personally think that’s a strange choice to give an Asian dragon in a movie that seems to be set in a fantasy version of Asia a Finnish name.
Sisu is also a Gayonese word of Gayo people who live in highland of Aceh, Sumatra, Indonesia, which is a Southeast Asian country. Sisu means ‘whisper’ in Gayonese.
I think it looks similar to Sang Hyang Antaboga (a Javanese mythology) and some people saying that Sisu is also a combination of some animals that are native from Southeast Asia.

@ohkeydokeythen I mean, they're chimeral dragon-horses or koi-horses. Qilin/Kirin are separate from dragons in Asian folklore, and are thought to be closer to Unicorns due to being classed as lin (one horned beasts). However, they share the same elemental connection that Dragons have, so you could say they are close in this regard. 

Fun fact, much like the Questing Beast in European folklore, a depiction of a giraffe was mistaken as a real life Quilin

It being criticized is inevitable, as any other film. However, this is being even more criticized as its taken the route of Asian representation which we do not often have. To finally have a film about one of us made by a globally influential filmmaking studio like Disney is a big deal- the impact it’s going to have to millions of children all around the world would be great. More exposure to culture and finally- representation. It’s easy for people who don’t really have to deal with stuff like this to say we’re ‘overthinking’ it. Sorry, we are.

I love that this movie is a representation of Southeast Asians, but I really wish Disney made more of an effort to cast Southeast Asians in the main cast instead of a majority East Asians. As many of my SE Asian friends have discussed, Disney is giving us crumbs and we have to accept it as sufficient representation.

I love Daniel Dae Kim and Awkwafina and Gemma Chan, but they have had their representation in East Asian films. Many people especially in America still have trouble distinguishing Southeast Asians from East Asians, and this casting is really disrespectful to SE Asians who have been misrepresented by these interpretations all their lives.

The Word of The Lord Spoken to Timothy
For All Those Who Have Ears to Hear


I am The Lord your God who brought His people out of Egypt. I am The Lord God of Heaven and Earth. I am THE GOD; there is none besides Me. Behold, I came down to you in the flesh, and many beheld My face in The Redeemer. And now I am come again,[1] even to the outpouring of My spirit on all flesh.

Yet you of this world will not receive Me! Therefore I am sending out many in My own name, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to prepare My way before Me. Yet you shall persecute them, and they shall be despised by all nations. Behold, a great hissing shall arise even among My own people, and many mocking voices shall come forth from among those who call of themselves Christian and Jew. Even from among the devout of Israel shall many step forward to stone them in word and by deed, for they do always walk in the ways of their forefathers. 
Yet undaunted shall My witnesses in turn speak louder, with the power of The Lord in their nostrils! - A whirlwind of words and proclamations, miraculous signs and wonders, all in the name of YahuShua The Messiah, to the astonishment of the people! They shall blow the Trumpet and call upon the name of The Lord, in judgment and sanctification alike, yet they shall not raise a hand against another in violence. For theirs is the war of words, and strong shall the word of their mouth be. 
For as it was in the days of Noah, so shall the coming of The Son of Man be; as it was in Egypt, so shall it be in the Day of Preparation: A time of judgment and wrath, plus one hundred fold. As it was in all days past when I sent out My prophets, showing mighty signs and wonders, so shall it be at this time also. 
Beware, for the evil one shall attempt the same. Yet believe not these prophets, nor listen to their subtle and dark speeches. Neither believe your eyes, though you behold their false signs and wonders - trickery, dark sentences, the power of the pit, deceptions clothed in falsehoods with a veneer of light and truth.


Therefore have understanding and seek wisdom, 
Know who I have sent and who I am sending...

The prophets, My witnesses, 144,000, 
Who cease not from glorifying My name day and night...

Gifts and wonders without price, 
All in the name of The One True God 
And YahuShua The Messiah, The Risen One.


Therefore, My people, unless His name be called out in truth, by no means shall one tittle come to pass or be accounted as true. Turn your back on all who say, “Peace, peace,” when there is no peace! And run from those who say, “There are no more prophets of God,” as they subvert whole households, teaching things which they ought not for the sake of dishonest gain! Run, I say, and do not look back! Be wary and vigilant.
And to all those who dwell in the churches of men, I say this: Pray, and open not your mouths; pray and be quiet, and open not your mouths; pray and give heed, for the mouth of The Living God has spoken. Discern the spirit of those I am sending, and listen to those I have sent already. Speak not one word against them, not in open, nor in secret. Speak not one word against them, for you speak against Me; I have sent them! Behold, they carry nothing of their own, they have taken nothing with them. For I have put My words in their mouths, My trumpet has been placed in their hands, and by the power of My own spirit shall I establish them; it is I who overshadows them. 
These are they who are not defiled by the corrupt doctrines and filthy traditions of the churches of men. Indeed, they stand apart from every religion, and remain spiritually undefiled, men of valor bought for a price, set apart for My purpose. Thus they shall take of Mine, and through the power of My spirit show it to you. For that which is Mine is The Son’s also. And that which is His He shall share with those who walk in union with Him, that I may be glorified in Him and He in them.

Therefore, thus says The Lord to the churches of men and to all who lead them: You shall stay silent and open not your mouths. And you shall give heed to My every decree, and now strive to obey ALL My Commandments. Yet forbear and speak, and I Myself shall come against you, on a day you did not look for and at an hour you did not expect, striking you dumb, leaving you mute for a whole season, abased in the sight of many witnesses! 
Thus those who were full shall be left empty, so they may be truly filled up, and those who were thought of as first shall be last. Yet those who were forsaken as last shall be first partakers of The Glory. And those without knowledge shall be given great knowledge, for Love has saved them. Yet those who had attained great knowledge shall be brought down to shame, so they too may be glorified in My presence, for Love has saved them.


Therefore let it be written and understood, 
For it shall surely come to pass:

Nothing, nothing in all creation, 
Can usurp the will of The Most High God! 
Nor shall any run out ahead of Me...

MY will shall be done on earth as it is in Heaven, 
For it is purposed and can not be moved, 
Having been written in everlasting stone 
From the very foundation of the world...

Here’s something interesting, the design of the dragon reminds me of a specific style used to depict dragons in the Vietnamese historical period Lý: elongated, graceful body and big heads and eyes, with wavy cloud-like fins. Personally, I’m South East Asian and I am really looking forward to this!

The "dragon" is horrible. I  get that it's supposed to be some kind of runt, a goofy dragon, but come on. It looks like it's from an entirely different movie, the art styles don't match at all compared to the rest of the world and the characters. It's grey toned, muted, and the faces are "realistic" enough for a Disney movie, but meanwhile the dragon looks like it came right out of a My Little Pony animation. It looks like a weird mix of an oversaturated unicorn from a kid's show (like ages 3-5) and a puppy snout. The eyes are the worst part. It just looks odd. I wish they had gone for a more... mature? Look. Smaller eyes for the dragon, a more majestic appearance. Not a lot, just a little. And don't even get me started on the modern humor of the group projects. Is this movie for little kids (around 5 years) or older children and adults? Because the majority of the artstyle and the story, characters etc, seem mature and grown up, a bit more realistic than, say, Frozen. But then there's the dragon and I'm just.... ???

On the contrary. Human beings are inherently good, just limited in inclusivity. We are evolving beyond that, though; slowly and with stumbling steps. How do I know this is so? Food banks, charities, all the little things that let people help other people. @rage_tv: “...pretty sure the whole “trust” thing doesn’t fit well...” the reason we even have a society, let alone towns, cities, states, nations, is because we’ve been able to develop levels of trust between us. At some point, without any trust, we’d all be living in singular hovels, greedily holding on to the few crumbs we have. It really dawned on me that Disney basically have this market for animated girl movies. "From the creators of Moana and Frozen", comes this new movie - the mash between Moana and Frozen (lady with a darker skin complexion and a frost -princess- dragon). Of course there is the obvious Mulan reference. But wouldn't it be cool if they make a boy animated movie? When was the last time they did that? Hercules? Atlantis? Aladdin?

Come on, guys! There is a market for both. No need to ostracize little boys just because Disney wants to be progressive. Also you could have avoided this movie being Mulan 2.0 by simply making the main character a boy... just saying. I have watched if not all, most of the Disney princess movies. I like them. But you run out of ideas, lol. Didn't someone at the studio mention, that you guys already have a chinese animated princess, who has a dragon sidekick? Or should we go along with the baby being the unique feature here? Hopefully you don't make it wise and helpful like a certain well known cricket.

That being said, the new Mulan looks good enough. Unfortunately, I think it will go down in history as just that - "The new Mulan, a combination of Frozen and Moana. The true remake of Mulan, which the movie adaptation failed to do."

For those who ask for the con-baby: "The Tiyanak (also Tianak or Tianac) is a vampiric creature in Philippine mythology that takes on the form of a toddler or baby. Although there are various types, it typically takes the form of a newborn baby and cries in the jungle to attract unwary travelers. Once it is picked up by an unfortunate passerby, it reverts to its true form and attacks the victim. The tiyanak is also depicted to take malevolent delight in leading travelers astray, or in abducting children."
